explanations.25: Children.forEach is a React utility that safely loops through child elements, even if there's only one child or if some children are null.
explanations.28: isValidElement checks if something is actually a React component (not plain text), so we can safely call methods on it.
explanations.62: useRef creates a box that holds a value (like a timer ID) without causing re-renders when it changes—useful for cleanup tasks.
explanations.64: This cleanup function runs when the component unmounts, clearing any leftover timeout so we don't try to update a component that no longer exists.
explanations.71: useMemo caches the result of parsing children so we don't re-parse every time the component re-renders; it only re-parses if files or children actually change.
explanations.81: processChildren is defined inside useMemo and called recursively to handle nested React components (like when a file is wrapped in extra divs).
explanations.85: We check both child.type === Guideline and child.type?.isGuideline because the component reference might be wrapped or transformed by build tools.
explanations.96: This logic distinguishes between actual file components (Snippet or things with a filename) and regular HTML tags like <div> that just happen to have props.
explanations.106: The code prop is prioritized because a plugin may have already processed the file and injected its content there, rather than as raw children text.
explanations.132: We use a custom delimiter string in a bash here-document so we can safely paste files with special characters without breaking the shell command.
explanations.147: navigator.clipboard.writeText copies text to the user's clipboard—a modern way to handle copy-to-clipboard without Flash plugins.
explanations.150: We store the timeout ID in copiedTimerRef so the cleanup function in useEffect can cancel it if the component unmounts before 2 seconds pass.
explanations.163: We strip leading slashes (like /tmp/folder) because zip file paths shouldn't start with /, which would cause issues on Windows and some zip tools.
explanations.178: zip.generateAsync({ type: 'blob' }) converts the in-memory zip into a downloadable file blob; await waits for that conversion to complete.
explanations.184: We append the link to the DOM before clicking because some browsers (like older Firefox) require the element to be in the document for the download to work.
